Title: Structural assembly of maize CRY-GL2 photosignaling complex provides insights into its regulatory role in cuticular wax biosynthesis

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: 3-ketoacyl-CoA synthase (KCS), condensing enzyme of VLCFA elongase
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Cuticular wax biosynthesis; very-long-chain fatty acid (VLCFA) elongation
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Fatty acid elongase condensation module for >C28 VLCFA production
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Condenses a long-chain acyl-CoA with malonyl-CoA to form a 3-ketoacyl-CoA extended by two carbons during VLCFA elongation
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms VLCFA elongation intermediate required for wax precursor production
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Elongation/condensation step, midstream and iterative branch-defining step
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control step not directly established for CER6 alone here; activity depends on complex with ZmGL2
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Forms protective cuticle for environmental adaptation and surface barrier function
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: ZmCER6 forms an active enzyme complex with ZmGL2 to catalyze VLCFA elongation required for cuticular wax biosynthesis; its activity is inhibited when ZmCRY1c competes for ZmGL2 binding
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Structural comparison with companion ZmCER6-ZmGL2 complex; in vitro enzyme assay with LC-MS detection of 32:0-ketoacyl-CoA; competitive inhibition assay by ZmCRY1c
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ZmCER6 forms an enzyme complex with ZmGL2 that is essential for VLCFA elongation activity. 2. The complex produces 32:0-ketoacyl-CoA from 30:0-CoA and malonyl-CoA in vitro. 3. ZmCRY1c competitively binds ZmGL2 and dose-dependently suppresses ZmCER6-ZmGL2 activity, linking light signaling to wax metabolism.
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Endoplasmic reticulum-associated wax elongation complex; exact localization inferred from discussion
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: ZmGL2; ZmCRY1c (competitive indirect effect via ZmGL2)
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Blue light; in vitro enzyme assay conditions
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target for engineering wax deposition and surface lipid traits, especially together with regulators controlling ZmGL2 availability under light
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Likely altered cuticle properties and associated drought/pathogen interactions if flux is changed; not tested in planta
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper’s main experiments center on how ZmCRY1c regulates the ZmCER6-ZmGL2 complex; CER6 mechanistic architecture is partly supported by a companion paper rather than fully resolved here.

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Cryptochrome 1 family flavoprotein; photolyase homology region (PHR)
Regulation_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Nucleus and cytoplasm; colocalizes with ZmGL2 in cytoplasm
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Light-dependent protein-protein interaction and competitive inhibition of metabolic enzyme complex assembly
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Forms light-activated homotetrameric scaffold and 4:4 ZmCRY1c-ZmGL2 hetero-octameric complex
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: VLCFA elongation efficiency during cuticular wax biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: ZmGL2; indirectly the ZmCER6-ZmGL2 enzyme complex
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Binds ZmGL2 and competitively displaces/blocks ZmCER6 association, thereby reducing ZmCER6-ZmGL2 enzymatic activity
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Blue light; photoreduction/FAD-dependent activation; oligomerization state
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Protective cuticle component aiding environmental adaptation and surface barrier function
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Light-activated ZmCRY1c acts as a molecular switch that inhibits VLCFA elongation by competing with ZmCER6 for ZmGL2 binding, thereby potentially reducing wax biosynthetic efficiency
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Condition-dependent controller linking blue-light perception to cuticular wax biosynthesis through direct competition within a metabolic complex
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Crystal structure; ITC; photoreduction assay; mutagenesis; pull-down; yeast light-responsive interaction assay; in vitro LC-MS enzyme inhibition assay
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Activated ZmCRY1c directly binds ZmGL2 in a light-dependent manner. 2. ZmCRY1c oligomerization creates the ZmGL2-binding interface and forms a 4:4 hetero-octameric complex. 3. ZmCRY1c competitively inhibits the ZmCER6-ZmGL2 complex and suppresses VLCFA elongation in a dose-dependent manner.
Regulation_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: ZmGL2; ZmCER6 (competition through shared ZmGL2 interface)
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Blue light illumination (30 or 50 μmol m−2 s−1); dark controls
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: A candidate optoregulatory lever for tuning wax deposition and environmental adaptation traits by manipulating light-responsive repression of VLCFA elongation
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Altered wax levels may affect water retention, pathogen resistance, and surface properties; in planta effects not validated
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Interaction appears maize-specific; Arabidopsis CRY2/CER2 homologs did not interact. W368A is a constitutively active mimic used for structural work; D381A abolishes FAD-dependent activation and GL2 binding.

Regulation_Genes.Regulator_Type: BAHD acyltransferase family metabolic regulator/scaffold protein
Regulation_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Nucleus and cytoplasm; likely associated functionally with ER-localized elongation system via ZmCER6 complex
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Protein complex formation that modulates enzyme substrate tunnel and VLCFA elongation; subject to competitive inhibition by photoreceptor binding
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Forms ZmCER6-ZmGL2 heterotetramer and ZmCRY1c-ZmGL2 hetero-octamer
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: VLCFA elongation and cuticular wax biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Associates with ZmCER6 to enable/boost elongation of >C28 acyl chains; binding by ZmCRY1c interferes with this positive role
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Blue-light-activated ZmCRY1c competes for GL2 binding
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Protective cuticle component aiding environmental adaptation and barrier formation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: ZmGL2 is a central positive regulator/scaffold of the ZmCER6 elongase complex for VLCFA elongation, but its activity is antagonized by light-activated ZmCRY1c binding
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Essential positive controller of the VLCFA elongation module whose occupancy by either ZmCER6 or ZmCRY1c determines wax-elongation efficiency
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Crystal structure with ZmCRY1c; ITC; pull-down; mutagenesis; yeast optical switch assay; referenced companion structural/enzyme evidence with ZmCER6
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ZmGL2 directly binds activated ZmCRY1c through a defined N-terminal interface. 2. ZmGL2 also forms an essential enzyme complex with ZmCER6 for VLCFA elongation. 3. Overlapping interfaces allow ZmCRY1c to compete with ZmCER6, creating a light-responsive switch in wax metabolism.
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Blue light for CRY-dependent interaction assays
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong target for modifying wax chain-length profile and deposition by altering interaction surfaces with ZmCER6 or ZmCRY1c
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Changing GL2 interactions may broadly alter surface lipid composition and stress responses; not tested in planta
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although annotated as BAHD acyltransferase family, in this study ZmGL2 functions primarily as a metabolic regulatory/scaffolding factor rather than as a directly assayed acyltransferase catalyst.
